content.problemStatement:
Chef has to travel to another place. For this, he can avail any one of two cab services.


The first cab service charges XXX rupees.
The second cab service charges YYY rupees.
Chef wants to spend the **minimum** amount of money. Which cab service should Chef take?


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers XXX and YYY - the prices of first and second cab services respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output `FIRST` if the first cab service is cheaper, output `SECOND` if the second cab service is cheaper, output `ANY` if both cab services have the same price.


You may print each character of `FIRST`, `SECOND` and `ANY` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `any`, `aNy`, `Any` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y, the prices of the first and second cab services respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: FIRST if X < Y, SECOND if X > Y, or ANY if X = Y.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 30 < 65, so the first cab is cheaper → FIRST.\nTest case 2: 42 = 42, both cost the same → ANY.\nTest case 3: 90 > 50, so the second cab is cheaper → SECOND.

solution.approach:
For each test case read the two integers X and Y. Compare them:
- If X < Y, the first cab is cheaper → print "FIRST".
- If X > Y, the second cab is cheaper → print "SECOND".
- Otherwise they cost the same → print "ANY".
The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
